engineGuide.h2: Aston Martin DB6 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Aston Martin DB6 across all production years, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: The iconic Tadek Marek twin-cam straight-six defined the DB6; aluminium construction, DOHC valvetrain, and SU carburettors delivered smooth grand touring character.
compatibleVariants: DB6 Saloon Standard, DB6 Volante, DB6 Mk1, DB6 Early Production, DB6 Right-Hand Drive UK, DB6 Left-Hand Drive Export
commonFailures: Oil pressure drop from main bearing wear - typically evident after 60k miles
Timing chain stretch causing cam timing drift and top-end rattle
Cooling system marginal for sustained high-speed use; big-grille upgrade recommended
entries.cta: Get quotes for Marek L6 Aston Martin DB6 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L High-Compression Petrol
entries.history: The Vantage specification raised compression to 10.5:1 and fitted triple Weber 45DCOE carburettors, transforming the DB6 into a genuine performance collector car.
compatibleVariants: DB6 Vantage Saloon, DB6 Vantage Volante, DB6 Competition Specification, DB6 Weber Carburettor Specification
commonFailures: High-compression detonation on lower-octane fuel - requires premium pump fuel or racing blend
Triple Weber synchronization drift causing uneven fueling and flooding
Valve guide wear accelerated by performance use; leads to oil consumption
entries.cta: Get quotes for Marek L6-Vantage Aston Martin DB6 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.2L Enlarged Capacity Petrol
entries.history: Introduced for Mk2 and select Vantage models, the enlarged 4.2L displacement offered increased torque while retaining the Marek straight-six's refined character.
compatibleVariants: DB6 Mk2 Saloon, DB6 Mk2 Volante, DB6 Late Production, DB6 Vantage (optional enlargement)
commonFailures: Cylinder liner seating issues if not properly secured during rebuild
Oil pump wear from increased displacement demand; reduces idle pressure
SU/Weber compatibility confusion on transitional Mk2 units
entries.cta: Get quotes for Marek L6-4.2 Aston Martin DB6 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Aston Martin DB6 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Aston Martin DB6's Tadek Marek straight-six is a robust grand touring engine, but oil pressure drop, timing chain wear, and cooling limitations are the three most prominent failure patterns driving replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. The problem groups below help you decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your DB6.

problems.h4: Oil Pressure Drop - Why DB6 Main Bearings Wear Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB6 Saloon, Volante, Mk1, Mk2 (1965-1970, Marek L6 all codes)
problems.rootCause: Original main bearing clearances and oil pump capacity were marginal for sustained high-speed use; wear accelerates if oil change intervals are extended or incorrect viscosity used.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Oil System Refresh - Pump replacement, filter upgrade, flush
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil pump, fit high-flow filter, flush galleries, refill with correct 20W-50 mineral oil
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may restore pressure for 10-20k miles if bearing wear is minimal

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Bearing Replacement - Bottom-end strip, new bearings, clearances checked
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove sump, inspect crank, fit oversized main/rod bearings, reassemble with updated clearances
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if crank is undamaged; requires expert machining and assembly

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Marek L6 Engine - Complete rebuild with upgraded components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of bench-tested rebuilt engine with upgraded bearings, oil pump, and cooling provisions; includes 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution for high-mileage or performance-use DB6; eliminates recurrence risk

problems.recommendation: On a DB6 valued at £80,000-£250,000, investing in a full rebuilt engine with upgraded bearings is often more cost-effective than repeated partial repairs. If oil pressure is only slightly low and the engine is otherwise sound, a specialist bearing replacement may suffice - but get a compression test first.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Marek L6 engine prices for your Aston Martin DB6

problems.h4: Timing Chain Stretch - Twin-Cam DB6 Top-End Rattle & Failure Risk
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB6 all variants (1965-1970, Marek L6 twin-cam codes)
problems.rootCause: Original chain tensioners use friction pads that wear; stretched chain causes cam timing drift, valve interference risk, and distinctive top-end rattles on cold start.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioners, guides; reset cam timing; verify valve clearance
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended preventative maintenance; extends top-end life by 30-50k miles if done before failure

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Marek L6 with Updated Timing Components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of rebuilt engine with upgraded chain, tensioners, and verified cam timing; bench-tested before dispatch
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if chain has already jumped or caused valve damage; eliminates repeat risk

problems.recommendation: If you hear a cold-start rattle that disappears when warm, budget for a preventative chain replacement immediately - waiting risks catastrophic valve damage. On a collector DB6, a full rebuilt engine with updated timing components offers peace of mind and preserves value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for DB6 timing chain replacement or rebuilt engine supply

problems.h4: Overheating Under Load - DB6 Cooling System Limitations on High-Speed Touring
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB6 all variants (1965-1970, Marek L6 all codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage; triggered by sustained high-speed driving or hot ambient conditions
problems.rootCause: Original radiator capacity and airflow management were marginal for sustained 100+ mph touring; Kamm-tail aerodynamics reduce rear cooling airflow.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Cooling System Upgrade - Big-grille radiator, improved fan, thermostat
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit high-capacity aluminium radiator, upgraded fan clutch, lower-temp thermostat, flush system
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for most road-use DB6; reduces operating temperature by 10-15°C

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Cooling Overhaul - Radiator, water pump, hoses, oil cooler
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace radiator, water pump, all hoses, add oil cooler; pressure-test system
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage or performance-use cars; addresses multiple weak points

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Enhanced Cooling Provisions
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of rebuilt engine with upgraded cooling passages, compatible with big-grille radiator and oil cooler
repairOptions.longevity: Best for cars used on fast tours or in hot climates; prevents recurrence of heat-related damage

problems.recommendation: For a DB6 used primarily for classic car events and moderate-speed touring, a cooling system upgrade is usually sufficient. If the engine has already suffered heat-related damage (warped head, blown gasket), a rebuilt engine with enhanced cooling provisions is the prudent investment.
problems.cta: Compare DB6 cooling upgrade and rebuilt engine options UK

problems.h4: Weber Carburettor Flooding - Vantage-Spec Fuel System Tuning Challenges
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB6 Vantage, Vantage Volante, Weber-spec variants (1965-1970, Marek L6-Vantage codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage; triggered by incorrect tuning, fuel quality, or temperature changes
problems.rootCause: Triple Weber 45DCOE carburettors require precise synchronization and jetting; temperature changes or ethanol-blended fuel can cause flooding, lean running, or uneven fueling.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip, clean, rebuild Webers; flow-bench synchronization; jetting for current fuel; dyno verification
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if carbs are mechanically sound; requires specialist with Weber expertise

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Vantage Engine with Calibrated Fuel System
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of rebuilt Vantage-spec engine with freshly calibrated triple Webers; bench-tested under load
repairOptions.longevity: Best for collector Vantage models; ensures authentic performance and reliability

problems.recommendation: Weber tuning is an art - if your Vantage runs poorly after a generic service, seek a specialist with proven DB6 Vantage experience. For ultra-rare Vantage Volante models, investing in a fully calibrated rebuilt engine preserves both performance and provenance.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DB6 Vantage engine prices with Weber calibration

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Aston Martin DB6
finalCta.paragraph: The DB6's Tadek Marek straight-six is a masterpiece of grand touring engineering, but oil pressure drop, timing chain wear, and cooling limitations are well-documented failure patterns that can escalate quickly. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK DB6 specialists who understand these classic engines. Every rebuilt unit includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - with nationwide delivery or supply & fit available. Compare quotes today and keep your DB6 on the road without the main-dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Aston Martin DB6 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Aston Martin DB6 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Aston Martin DB6 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine specifications across the Aston Martin DB6 production timeline (1965-1970), organised so owners can quickly identify their Marek L6 engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The DB6 launched with the Tadek Marek 4.0L twin-cam straight-six, establishing the engine specification that would define the model throughout its production life.
keyChanges: DB6 replaces DB5; new Kamm-tail aerodynamics and longer wheelbase
Marek L6 4.0L twin-cam introduced with SU HD8 carburettors as standard
Vantage specification announced with triple Weber 45DCOE and 10.5:1 compression
mainEngines: Marek L6 / DB6-4.0 - 4.0L petrol (Saloon Standard, early Volante)
Marek L6-Vantage / DB6-V - 4.0L high-compression petrol (Vantage Saloon)
knownFor: Early casting numbers critical for numbers-matching restoration
SU carburettors simpler to maintain than Weber but less performance-oriented
Oil pressure monitoring essential; early bearing wear patterns established
Cooling system adequate for road use but marginal for sustained high-speed touring
years.cta: Get quotes for 1965-1966 Aston Martin DB6 replacement engines
years.ticker: DB6 oil pressure drop fix • DB6 timing chain wear replacement • DB6 SU carburettor tuning

years.description: Mid-production DB6 models retained the core Marek L6 specification while receiving incremental running changes to ancillaries and cooling provisions.
keyChanges: Minor running changes to ancillary brackets and cooling hose routing
Volante convertible production ramped up; chassis-specific engine mounting refinements
Vantage specification remained low-volume, hand-built option
knownFor: Casting number verification critical for mid-production compatibility
Timing chain tensioner wear becoming evident on higher-mileage examples
Big-grille cooling upgrade gaining popularity among high-speed touring owners
SU vs Weber carburettor expertise increasingly specialised among UK rebuilders
years.cta: Get quotes for 1967-1968 Aston Martin DB6 replacement engines

years.description: The Mk2 update introduced the enlarged 4.2L Marek L6 option alongside the established 4.0L, while Vantage specification remained available with either displacement.
keyChanges: DB6 Mk2 announced with longer wheelbase and optional 4.2L engine enlargement
Fuel injection (AE Brico) offered on rare Mk2 Vantage specification
Final production year 1970; DBS begins to supersede DB6 in Aston Martin lineup
mainEngines: Marek L6 / DB6-4.0 - 4.0L petrol (late Saloon, Volante)
Marek L6-4.2 / DB6-4.2 - 4.2L petrol (Mk2 Saloon, Mk2 Volante, optional Vantage)
Marek L6-Vantage / DB6-V - 4.0L or 4.2L high-compression petrol (Vantage, Vantage Volante)
knownFor: 4.2L enlargement requires careful cylinder liner seating during rebuild
Fuel injection Brico system exceptionally rare; specialist advice essential
Late-production casting numbers differ from early units; verification recommended
Mk2 chassis mounting points subtly different; confirm fitment before ordering replacement
years.cta: Get quotes for 1969-1970 Aston Martin DB6 replacement engines
years.ticker: DB6 Mk2 4.2L engine replacement • DB6 Vantage Volante engine supply • DB6 fuel injection specialist rebuild

faq.h2: Aston Martin DB6 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does an Aston Martin DB6 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Marek L6 engines for the DB6 range from £8,500-£22,000 supply-only, depending on specification (Standard vs Vantage), displacement (4.0L vs 4.2L), and whether upgraded components addressing known failure points are included.
keyPoints: Standard 4.0L Saloon/Volante rebuilt: £8,500-£15,000 supply-only
Vantage-spec high-compression rebuilt: £12,000-£28,000 supply-only
Supply & fit service adds £2,500-£5,500 depending on location and ancillary work
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Aston Martin DB6 engine prices UK

items.question: What is the most common engine problem on the Aston Martin DB6?
items.answer: Oil pressure drop from main bearing wear is the most frequently reported failure on DB6 Marek L6 engines, typically becoming evident after 50,000-80,000 miles if oil change intervals are extended or incorrect viscosity used.
keyPoints: Main bearing wear reduces oil pressure; warning light may illuminate under load
Correct 20W-50 mineral oil with ZDDP additive essential for bearing protection
Upgraded bearings and oil pump included in quality rebuilt engines prevent recurrence
items.cta: Get quotes for DB6 oil pressure repair or rebuilt engine supply

items.question: Can I upgrade my DB6 4.0L engine to 4.2L capacity?
items.answer: Yes, the 4.2L Marek L6 is a feasible upgrade for 4.0L DB6 models, but requires careful cylinder liner seating, compatible ancillaries, and specialist tuning to ensure reliability and retain authentic character.
keyPoints: 4.2L conversion increases torque; beneficial for heavier Volante or touring use
Cylinder liners must be properly secured to prevent shifting under load
SU/Weber carburettor jetting and ignition timing require recalibration for displacement change
items.cta: Enquire about Aston Martin DB6 4.2L engine conversion specialists

items.question: Do DB6 Vantage engines require special maintenance?
items.answer: Yes, Vantage-spec Marek L6 engines with 10.5:1 compression and triple Weber carburettors demand high-octane fuel, expert synchronization, and more frequent valve clearance checks than standard DB6 engines.
keyPoints: High-compression ratio requires premium pump fuel or racing blend to prevent detonation
Triple Weber 45DCOE carburettors need specialist synchronization every 10,000 miles
Valve clearance checks recommended every 5,000 miles on performance-spec engines
items.cta: Compare rebuilt DB6 Vantage engine prices with Weber calibration

items.question: How long does a DB6 engine rebuild take in the UK?
items.answer: Typical turnaround for a quality DB6 Marek L6 rebuild is 8-16 weeks, depending on parts availability, specification complexity (Standard vs Vantage), and whether ancillary refurbishment is included.
keyPoints: Standard 4.0L rebuild: 8-12 weeks typical if core components available
Vantage-spec or 4.2L rebuild: 12-16 weeks due to specialised parts and calibration
Bench testing and warranty documentation add 1-2 weeks to final delivery
items.cta: Get timeline estimate for your Aston Martin DB6 engine rebuild

items.question: What warranty comes with a reconditioned DB6 engine?
items.answer: Reconditioned or rebuilt DB6 engines supplied through EngineMarket's UK network include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects, with some specialists offering 24-month terms on concours-spec rebuilds.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network
Warranty covers parts and labour for defects in rebuild workmanship
Always confirm exact terms, exclusions, and claim process with chosen specialist before ordering
items.cta: Compare warranty terms for Aston Martin DB6 rebuilt engines

items.question: Can a DB6 engine be fitted with electronic ignition?
items.answer: Yes, electronic ignition conversion is a popular upgrade for DB6 Marek L6 engines, improving reliability, cold starting, and reducing maintenance compared to original points ignition, while retaining authentic appearance if desired.
keyPoints: Electronic conversion eliminates points wear and timing drift issues
Hidden conversions retain original distributor appearance for concours authenticity
Improved spark energy benefits high-compression Vantage engines especially
items.cta: Enquire about electronic ignition upgrade for your DB6 engine rebuild

items.question: Is a DB6 engine compatible with unleaded petrol?
items.answer: Yes, but original DB6 engines with soft valve seats require hardened valve seat inserts or lead replacement additive to prevent valve recession when using modern unleaded fuel regularly.
keyPoints: Hardened valve seat inserts recommended during rebuild for unleaded compatibility
Lead replacement additive is a temporary solution; not ideal for high-mileage use
Most quality rebuilt DB6 engines now include hardened seats as standard upgrade
items.cta: Confirm unleaded compatibility when ordering your DB6 rebuilt engine

items.question: How do I identify my DB6 engine specification?
items.answer: DB6 engine identification relies on casting numbers on the block and cylinder head, engine serial number plate, and carburettor type (SU vs Weber); our free registration lookup or specialist verification service can confirm exact specification.
keyPoints: Casting numbers located on block side and cylinder head; photograph for verification
Engine serial plate typically on timing cover or sump rail; may be obscured by grime
SU HD8 carbs indicate standard spec; triple Weber 45DCOE indicates Vantage specification
items.cta: Use our free registration lookup to identify your DB6 engine code instantly

items.question: Should I rebuild or replace my DB6 engine?
items.answer: For collector-grade or high-value DB6 models, a full rebuilt engine with upgraded components addressing known failure points typically offers better long-term value and reliability than repeated partial repairs.
keyPoints: Rebuild preserves original casting numbers for numbers-matching restoration
Upgraded bearings, oil pump, and timing components prevent recurrence of common failures
Bench testing and warranty on rebuilt engines reduce post-installation risk
items.cta: Compare rebuild vs replace cost options for your Aston Martin DB6
